mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate earnings expectations, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also high, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are above their respective 5-year moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, an acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust investment and consumer confidence, which serves as a positive signal for short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of triggered instances during an economic expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating strong underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a weakening job market, which can quickly translate into reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where PAYEMS 3-month average growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low, it suggests an accommodative monetary policy supporting a robust economy, which is highly favorable for S&P 500 forward returns as it implies strong earnings growth and low discount rates over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when GDPC1 growth is above its 5-year average and FEDFUNDS is below its 5-year average for more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, any unexpected increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a more aggressive monetary tightening stance, which can shock markets and lead to immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25pp in a month
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS increases significantly during a period when CPIAUCSL is above its 10-year average.
